message:
perf(search): skip post-query ordering when only sort is requested

`requires_post_query_ordering` was over-triggering the in-memory
re-sort path: any time `args.sort` was `Some(...)` it returned
`true`, even when no client-side filters were active. But the
SQL-side ORDER BY already produces rows in the requested sort
order, so the follow-up `apply_client_filters` + `sort_*` pass on
the materialized `Vec` was pure overhead — recomputing the same
ordering the database just gave us.

The post-query path is only needed when `client_filters` is true.
That's the case where SQLite returned a superset (because some
predicates can't be expressed in the where clause) and the
remaining filters drop rows in a way that breaks the SQL ordering's
guarantees about contiguous keys, so we have to re-sort the
trimmed list. With no client filters, dropping rows isn't on the
table and the SQL ordering is canonical.

Fix: `requires_post_query_ordering` ignores `args` (renamed to
`_args` to silence the lint) and returns `client_filters` directly.

Test:
- Renamed `test_requires_post_query_ordering_only_for_client_-
  filters_or_explicit_sort` to drop the `or_explicit_sort` half.
- Updated the `args.sort = Some("updated")` case to assert
  `false` instead of `true`.
- The other branches (no sort/no filters → false; client filters
  with `desc_contains` → true) are unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
